spec_match.rationale: DevTools MCP verified the live grid computes gap through --grid-gap(var(--grid-density)) and the column bars compute widths through --col-width(...), rather than only displaying JS-simulated values.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP changed the column select and both sliders; labels, generated cell count, computed gap, computed cell height, and live code output all updated.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: The page surfaces unsupported fallback state and DevTools MCP verified both high and low control boundaries: 12 columns / density 6 / 120px and 2 columns / density 1 / 24px.

references.rationale: The see also section now links to the required chromestatus.com/feature/5179721933651968 entry.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, and button overrides keep background + color paired (CLAUDE.md rules 5 and 6).

summary: DevTools MCP on Chrome 148 verified /v139/css-custom-functions/responsive-grid/?verify=after-real-function-grid: the controls are labelled, the page links the required ChromeStatus entry, @function support checks the exact --grid-gap()/--col-width() contracts, 12 columns / density 6 / 120px produced 24 cells with observed gap 28px, 2 columns / density 1 / 24px produced 4 cells with observed gap 8px, column bars use live --col-width(...) output, console/network were clean, and /v139/css-custom-functions/conformance/?verify=responsive-after-real-functions reported 10 PASS / 0 FAIL.
